title: Misatos whisper of Shinji kun on the Wunder
display_title: Misatos whisper of Shinji kun on the Wunder
wikitext:
[]: Misato's whisper of "Shinji-kun" on the Wunder             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 3:16 am [Split from "Misato's relationship with Shinji in 3.0" . Better/more specific topic title suggestions welcome. - Monk] Has anyone pointed out that when Misato says "Shinji-kun", she whispers it? That was only for her to hear, not the bridge crew. Also, when Ritsuko mentions that fourth impact was stopped but by whom to Misato, her expression is one of anger, and my view is that the "Ship of Hope" (WUNDER) is a powerless dud and it's a reminder just like 14 years ago that Misato is powerless.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 3:27 am wiser3754 wrote: Also, when Ritsuko mentions that fourth impact was stopped but by whom to Misato, her expression is one of anger, and my view is that the "Ship of Hope" (WUNDER) is a powerless dud and it's a reminder just like 14 years ago that Misato is powerless.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 5:00 am Very interesting take in Misato's attitude wiser3754! I though that her expression was anger against Shinji for having nearly fucked up everything again, but your interpretation that her anger comes from having to completely rely on the EVA pilots to save them(Asuka saving the Wunder and Mari saving the world !) like 14 years ago, despite all her work to finally acquire a weapon seemingly able to let her actively participate in the fight, is a much more likely possibility. It must have awakened her old feelings of powerlessness! I'm still ambiguous about the moment she whispered Shinji's name though : it could be that she's worrying for him because she suspect that Gendo must have manipulated the hell out of the kid to put him in this position(even more if the Wunder was listening to Asuka's and Shinji's exchange in Lilith's Chamber) and can't express her concern aloud because of the crew's probable reaction... or it could be that she's supremely pissed off at Shinji for letting himself getting manipulated and forcing WILLE to repair his errors(especially if the Wunder was listening to Asuka's and Shinji's exchange in Lilith's Chamber!)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 28, 2013 9:02 am ElMariachi wrote: Very interesting take in Misato's attitude wiser3754! I though that her expression was anger against Shinji for having nearly fucked up everything again, but your interpretation that her anger comes from having to completely rely on the EVA pilots to save them(Asuka saving the Wunder and Mari saving the world !) like 14 years ago, despite all her work to finally acquire a weapon seemingly able to let her actively participate in the fight, is a much more likely possibility. It must have awakened her old feelings of powerlessness!

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 9:15 am Lennik wrote: Shinji doesn't have to earn everyone's forgiveness. Frankly, that's impossible. All he needs to earn is the forgiveness of himself and the people who were closest to him. In Wille, he really only needs Misato and Asuka's forgiveness, and Mari will probably have no problem forgiving him once he truly gets his act together. He just needs to know that at least they don't hate him, and then he'll be able to see some light at the end of his tunnel. But absolving himself of responsibility for his mistakes will not be the message Anno is trying to send. But everyone's forgiveness? Not gonna happen. And that's something he has to accept.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 2:39 pm But remember that the scene with the two people in the convenience store was the day after Shinji's arrival in Tokyo-3, he wasn't known yet, but few days after, the whole shool was in the known, and with it probably the rest of the city. Rumors travel extremely fast, in WILLE there is already Misato, Ritsuko, Asuka, Mari, Sakura, the Bridge Bunnies and the four soldiers that where pointing guns at him who know who Shinji is, only one of them need to speak about it during the pausing hours, of in permission, and the rumor will spread like a wildfire. The advantage is that Shinji doesn't have any distinguishing mark that would led him to be immediately recognizable, unlike Asuka and her eyepatch for example, but the name "Shinji Ikari"(ir just Ikari, since his father is non-other than Gendo). Maybe Shinji will have to change his surname, take Yui's maiden name and starting a new life as "Shinji Ayanami", beside that could mark the beginning of a brother-sister relationship with ReiQ, who unlike Rei II has the advantage that we are sure that she's not Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Fri Jun 28, 2013 6:58 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: I wouldn't say it's completely useless. But it is true that against Eva-13 and the Mark.09, it was just as useless as the giant gun emplacements and missile launchers that Nerv used to use before the timeskip. So basically despite feeling she's taken a huge step forward by acquiring the Wunder, really Misato is still in the same position she was in 14 years before.

[]:             Quote Post by Lennik » Sat Jun 29, 2013 11:25 pm ElMariachi wrote: Very interesting take in Misato's attitude wiser3754! I though that her expression was anger against Shinji for having nearly fucked up everything again, but your interpretation that her anger comes from having to completely rely on the EVA pilots to save them(Asuka saving the Wunder and Mari saving the world !) like 14 years ago, despite all her work to finally acquire a weapon seemingly able to let her actively participate in the fight, is a much more likely possibility. It must have awakened her old feelings of powerlessness!

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sun Jun 30, 2013 3:36 am Lennik wrote: Or, just as likely a possibility (and far more likely than the idea that she's internally cursing Shinji), she's just come to regret her poor handling of him and feels guilty. Which, unless I'm the only one seeing the remorse here, is more what the film's final scenes imply with her expression at the end.
